mission.title: Install llm-d Prefill/Decode Disaggregation
mission.description: Deploy GPT-OSS-120B using vLLM's Prefill/Decode disaggregation with NIXL for KV-cache transfer. P/D disaggregation separates prefill and decode phases onto specialized workers, reducing inter-token latency and improving interactivity for large models with long input sequences. Validated on 8xH200 clusters with InfiniBand and RoCE networking.

steps.title: Install prerequisite client tools
steps.description:
Ensure you have the required client tools installed:
- `helm` (v3+)
- `helmfile` (v0.169+)
- `kubectl` (v1.29+)
- `yq` (v4+)

See the [client setup guide](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/prereq/client-setup).

steps.title: Prepare cluster with RDMA networking
steps.description:
Ensure your cluster has:
1. 8 NVIDIA GPUs (H200 recommended) with RDMA via InfiniBand or RoCE between all pods
2. Gateway API CRDs and a gateway provider deployed
3. The monitoring stack installed

**For Intel HPU:** Requires Intel Gaudi2/3 with at least 2 cards.

See [infrastructure prerequisites](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/prereq/infrastructure).

steps.title: Create namespace and HuggingFace token secret
steps.description:
```bash
export NAMESPACE=llm-d-pd
kubectl create namespace ${NAMESPACE}

kubectl create secret generic llm-d-hf-token \
  --from-literal=HF_TOKEN=<your-hf-token> \
  -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

steps.title: Deploy the P/D stack
steps.description:
```bash
cd guides/pd-disaggregation
helmfile apply -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

**For Intel HPU deployments:**
```bash
helmfile apply -e hpu -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

**For specific gateway providers:**
```bash
helmfile apply -e kgateway -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

This deploys 4 TP=1 Prefill workers and 1 TP=4 Decode worker for `openai/gpt-oss-120b`.

steps.title: Install HTTPRoute
steps.description:
**For Istio or kgateway:**
```bash
kubectl apply -f httproute.yaml -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

**For GKE:**
```bash
kubectl apply -f httproute.gke.yaml -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check Helm releases:
```bash
helm list -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

Expected: `gaie-pd`, `infra-pd`, `ms-pd` all in deployed status.

Verify pods:
```bash
kubectl get all -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

Expected: 1 EPP pod, 1 gateway pod, 4 prefill pods, 1 decode pod (with 2 containers for NIXL sidecar).

resolution.summary: A successful deployment shows 3 Helm releases and all pods running including 4 prefill workers (TP=1) and 1 decode worker (TP=4) with NIXL KV-cache transfer enabled via RDMA.

uninstall.title: Destroy the helmfile deployment

upgrade.title: Update llm-d version
upgrade.description: Update the llm-d version in your helmfile configuration following the [version selection guide](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/prereq/client-setup#llm-d-version).

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade

troubleshooting.title: NIXL KV-cache transfer failures
troubleshooting.description:
If decode pods show transfer errors, verify RDMA networking:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/component=modelservice-decode -n ${NAMESPACE} --tail=100
```

Check that InfiniBand or RoCE is correctly configured between all pods. The decode pod should have 2 containers (vLLM + NIXL sidecar).

troubleshooting.title: Prefill/Decode ratio imbalance
troubleshooting.description: If prefill workers are saturated while decode is idle (or vice versa), adjust the xPyD ratio. Edit the model service values to change the number of prefill vs decode replicas based on your ISL/OSL ratio.

troubleshooting.title: Model too large for available GPUs
troubleshooting.description: GPT-OSS-120B requires significant GPU memory. Ensure your GPUs have sufficient VRAM. For H200 GPUs, 8 cards provide adequate memory with TP=4 decode and TP=1 prefill workers.

troubleshooting.title: Selective PD tuning
troubleshooting.description:
For short prompts where P/D overhead exceeds benefit, enable selective PD by setting the `threshold` value in the `pd-profile-handler` plugin:
```bash
cat gaie-pd/values.yaml | yq '.inferenceExtension.pluginsCustomConfig."pd-config.yaml"'
```

prerequisites.description: A Kubernetes cluster with 8 NVIDIA GPUs (H200 recommended), RDMA networking (InfiniBand or RoCE) between all pods, Gateway API CRDs, a gateway provider, monitoring stack, and a HuggingFace token.
